description: Invest in farmland and timberland for combined income from crop/lumber sales and land appreciation, with biological growth providing optionality on harvest timing

sources_of_return.income: Crop sales (row, permanent) or timber harvest
sources_of_return.biological_growth: Trees grow volume and value with age — defer-harvest optionality

farmland_specifics.row_crops: Annual rotation; price exposure to corn, soy, wheat
farmland_specifics.permanent_crops: Nuts, fruits; longer horizon, higher capex
farmland_specifics.leasing: Cash lease (fixed) or crop share (variable income)

timberland_specifics.harvest_optionality: Defer cutting in weak prices; value compounds via growth

inflation_hedging.rule: Both asset classes provide partial inflation hedge via commodity prices and land value

risks.commodity_prices: Farm income sensitive to spot agricultural prices
risks.political: Land-use rules, export controls, water rights

errors.message: land_type must be farmland, timberland, or raw_land

anti_patterns.rule: Do not ignore water-rights risk in farmland
anti_patterns.why: Western US and drought-prone regions face binding water curtailment; land without secure rights loses value fast

anti_patterns.rule: Do not model timberland as pure commodity bet
anti_patterns.why: Biological growth decouples returns from timber spot prices over long horizons; harvest timing flexibility is a key source of alpha

anti_patterns.rule: Do not use listed timber REITs as proxy for direct timberland volatility
anti_patterns.why: Listed vehicles carry equity market beta; direct volatility is far lower due to biological growth buffer
